title: GCT Through EML — Representation Theory Angle

analysis.object: T916: GCT Through EML — Representation Theory Angle depth analysis

gct_overview.description: GCT: use GL(n) representation theory to separate permanent from determinant. GL(n) rep theory = EML-3 (spectral, oscillatory, highest weight).

permanent_emlinf.description: Permanent: sum over all permutations = EML-inf (exponential enumeration). #P-complete. The function ITSELF is EML-inf.

determinant_eml2.description: Determinant: polynomial time algorithm (LU decomposition). Determinant = EML-2 function.

gct_as_depth_sep.description: GCT tries to prove: no EML-2 computation (det) can simulate EML-inf (perm). In EML language: depth separation between EML-2 and EML-inf.
gct_as_depth_sep.reason: GCT = depth separation EML-2 vs EML-inf

eml_accelerates_gct.description: EML framework provides: EML-2 ≠ EML-inf (structural, by definition of the hierarchy). If GCT can formalize THIS structural difference as an algebraic obstruction, GCT closes.
eml_accelerates_gct.reason: EML hierarchy gives GCT its obstruction

multiplicity_obstruction.description: GCT multiplicity obstruction: certain GL(n) representations appear in the permanent but not the determinant. These representations are EML-3 objects (highest weight modules).

t916_theorem.description: T916: GCT uses EML-3 representations to prove EML-2 (det) cannot simulate EML-inf (perm). The EML framework provides the depth separation GCT needs as a structural theorem. The multiplicity obstruction IS the EML-3 witness to the EML-2/inf gap. T916.
t916_theorem.reason: GCT = EML-3 witness to EML-2/inf gap

analysis.theorem: T916: GCT Through EML — Representation Theory Angle (S1196).

key_theorem: T916: GCT Through EML — Representation Theory Angle (S1196).
